LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS EDWIN SALAZAR, REEVE TRUCKING COMPANY, INC, OSCAR MATUS, LYDIA FUENTES' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES, TENTATIVE RULING DOPTED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT HIRING IS OVERRULED. COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND THAT IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. (SEE MINI-MINUTES ENTERED THIS DATE FOR FULL DETAILS OF THE COURT'S RULING) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; NOT REPORTED. (302/EPS)

DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-05-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS EDWIN SALAZAR, REEVE TRUCKING COMPANY, INC, OSCAR MATUS, LYDIA FUENTES' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendants' demurrer to the second cause of action for negligent hiring is overruled. The gist of the demurrer is that plaintiff improperly seeks punitive damages on the claim. Defendant fails to show that plaintiff cannot recover any damages and plaintiff states a claim for negligent hiring. A motion to strike is the proper vehicle to raise this issue. (See Beason v. Griff (1954) 127 Cal.App.2d 382, 387 ["The averment of an improper or wrong measure of damages, or the demand or prayer for items of damage not warranted by the facts alleged is not a ground for general demurrer."]; PH II, Inc. v. Superior Court (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1680, 1683 ["The motion to strike is widely used to challenge portions of causes of action seeking punitive damages."].)

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. (302/EPS)

Defendants Edwin Mendez Salazar Dba Terraba Trucking (Erroneously Sued Herein As Terraba Trucking) Reeve Trucking Company Inc Oscar Arsenio Matus And Lydia Fuentes Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-05-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT EDWN SALAZAR, REEVE TRUCKING COMPANY, INC, OSCAR MATUS, LYDIA FUENTES' Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint.

No appearances. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendants' motion to strike the claim for punitive damages on the second cause of action for negligent hiring is granted without leave to amend.

"The elements of a cause of action for wrongful death are a tort, such as negligence, and resulting death." (Lopez v. City of Los Angeles (2011) 196 Cal.App.4th 675, 684.) Plaintiff's second cause of action alleges that defendants negligently hired or trained driver Matus and that negligence caused decedent's death. (See First Amended Complaint, pars. 35-27.) Plaintiff is thus pursuing a wrongful death claim. (See Mendoza v. City of Los Angeles (1998) 66 Cal.App.4th 1333, 1336 [referring to wrongful death claim for negligent hiring]; Summers v. A.L. Gilbert Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 1155, 1159 [same]; Davis v. City of Sacramento (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 393, 399 [same].) A wrongful death action is statutory in origin and exists only so far and in favor of such person as the legislative power may declare. (See Barrett v. Superior Court (1990) 222 Cal.App.3d 1176, 1184.) "Punitive damages are not available, however, in a statutory wrongful death action." (Boeken v. Philip Morris USA, Inc. (2010) 48 Cal.4th 788, 796; see also Code Civ. Proc. sec. 377.61; Doak v. Superior Court for Los Angeles County (1968) 257 Cal.App.2d 825, 834-835 [punitive damages not recoverable in wrongful death action].) Plaintiff's prayer for exemplary damages is therefore improper and the Court strikes it. Plaintiff's citation to CRST, Inc. v. Sup. Crt. (2017) 11 Cal.App.5th 1255, 1260-1262 is off point as that case did not involve a death.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. (302/EPS)
